title: Dryopteris celsa
taxonomy: Dryopteris celsa is classified within the kingdom Plantae, specifically in the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida and order Polypodiales. At the family level, it is classified as Dryopteridaceae. The genus Dryopteris is a group of terrestrial ferns, and Dryopteris celsa is one of its species. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other ferns.
uses: Dryopteris celsa, a species of fern, has been used in traditional medicine in various cultures. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In some traditional remedies, the plant is used to treat conditions such as fever and rheumatism. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of using Dryopteris celsa for medicinal purposes. Its potential uses and benefits are still being studied by scientists and researchers.

intro: Dryopteris celsa, also known as the Caledonian golden-scaled fern, is a species of fern native to the Caledonian region of Scotland. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Dryopteris celsa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory conditions.
